bitfields.name: BOOT_FAIL_LED
bitfields.description: Assert on fatal errors during boot. ROM toggles the GPIO pin identified by this field whenever primary boot fails due to fatal errors before locking-up/reset. Note, use QUICK_SET_/CLR_GPIOx field to set the default level of pin. [4:0] GPIO Pin number [7:5] GPIO port number

bitfields.name: POWERDOWN_TIMEOUT_SECS
bitfields.description: Powerdown timeout: Timeout value in seconds. When a non-zero value is programmed in this field ROM uses it as idle timeout value to enter power-down state to conserve power

bitfields.name: ACL_SEC_0
bitfields.description: Access control for flash sector (offset range 0x00_0000 - 0x00_1FFF)

values.name: DEFAULT_RW_UNLOCKED
values.description: Data flash and unlocked (RW__). Default flash behavior with read and write access only. Instruction fetches (execute access) are blocked and generates security violation. Blocks assigned with this access control index can be changed to another index by software. This configuration register is unlocked and updatable by application

values.name: DATA_RW_LOCKED
values.description: Data flash and locked (RW_L). Read and write operations are allowed but execution is blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index cannot be changed to another index until next reset

values.name: ROM_RX_LOCKED
values.description: Read only Memory (ROM) and locked (R_XL). Read & execute operations are allowed but write is blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index cannot be changed to another index until next reset

values.name: DATA_ROM_LOCKED
values.description: Data Read only memory (DROM) and locked (R__L). Read operations is allowed but write & execute are blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index cannot be changed to another index until next reset

values.name: ROM_RX_UNLOCKED
values.description: Read-Only-Memory (ROM) and un-locked (R_X_). Read & execute operations are allowed but write is blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index can be changed to another index by software. This configuration register is unlocked and updatable by application

values.name: XOM_UNLOCKED
values.description: eXecute-only Memory (XOM) and un-locked (__X_). Execute operations are allowed but read & write are blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index can be changed to another index by software. This configuration register is unlocked and updatable by application

values.name: XOM_LOCKED
values.description: eXecute-Only-Memory (XOM) and locked (__XL). Execute operations are allowed but read & write are blocked. Blocks assigned with this access control index cannot be changed to another index until next reset

values.name: NO_ACCESS_LOCKED
values.description: Hidden (___L). Read, Write & execute operations are blocked. Once this access control index is assigned to a block, the memory range associated with block is hidden until next reset

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_0
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins high after reset. Each bit corresponds to the pin in GPIO port 0. When set ROM drives the corresponding pin high as soon as possible. By default most pins come-up as tri-stated inputs. This feature allows customer to specify active drive pins soon after reset instead of waiting till complete boot

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_0
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_1
registers.description: Drive GPIO 1 port pins high. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_1
registers.description: Drive GPIO 1 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: QUICK_SET_GPIO_2
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins high after reset. Each bit corresponds to the pin in GPIO port 0. When set ROM drives the corresponding pin high as soon as possible. By default most pins come-up as tri-stated inputs. This feature allows customer to specify active drive pins soon after reset instead of waiting till complete boot

registers.name: QUICK_CLR_GPIO_2
registers.description: Drive GPIO 0 port pins low. See description of QUICK_SET_GPIO_0 field

registers.name: ROP_STATE
registers.description: This fields allows the device lifecycle to be advanced to In-Field ROP1, In-Field ROP2, or In-Field ROP3 from Develop life cycle

bitfields.name: ROP_STATE
bitfields.description: This fields allows the device lifecycle to be advanced to In-Field ROP1, In-Field ROP2, or In-Field ROP3 from Develop life cycle

values.name: ROP_LEVEL2
values.description: ROP_STATE = 0x4939_8D8B. Debug disabled and locked, cannot be modified by customer, only limited debug mailbox commands available

values.name: ROP_LEVEL3
values.description: ROP_STATE = 0xB0AB_B703. Debug disabled and locked, cannot be modified by customer, no debug mailbox commands available

values.name: ROP_LEVEL1
values.description: ROP_STATE = 0xEEBA_04C3. Debug disabled and unlocked, can be modified by customer, only limited debug mailbox commands available

values.name: ROP_LEVEL0
values.description: ROP_STATE = 0xFFFF_FFFF (erased FLASH value), no ROP. Default for blank state

registers.name: SBL_PROT
registers.description: If the field contains a value other than 0x0000_0000 or 0xFFFF_FFFF, Debug Mailbox commands and ISP commands will not be allowed at In-Field ROP1 and In-Field life cycles

registers.name: SBL_START_ADDR
registers.description: At the end of boot ROM execution, if the user does not wish boot ROM to jump at address 0x0000_0000 (default jump address) of the main flash, this field needs to be programmed with a valid flash jump address

registers.name: Reserved 0x0009C
registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

values.name: ROP_LEVEL1

bitfields.name: SEC_BOOT_EN
bitfields.description: Secure boot enforcement. This field defines the minimum image verification procedure (CRC32, ECDSA p-256 sign). The Image type field in header indicates the type of verification data (checksum or signature) included in it

values.name: DISABLED
values.description: secure boot is disabled

values.name: ENABLED_1
values.description: secure boot is enabled

values.name: ENABLED_2
values.description: secure boot is enabled

values.name: ENABLED_3
values.description: secure boot is enabled

bitfields.name: LP_SEC_BOOT_EN
bitfields.description: Secure boot option for low power wake up from deep power down 1 & 2

values.name: Enabled_0
values.description: Same as cold boot (enforcement dictated by SEC_BOOT_EN), application address considered as SBL_START_ADDR or 0x0

values.name: Enabled_1
values.description: Same as cold boot (enforcement dictated by SEC_BOOT_EN), application address considered as SBL_START_ADDR or 0x0

values.name: Disabled
values.description: Faster wakeup path supported

values.name: Enabled_3
values.description: Same as cold boot (enforcement dictated by SEC_BOOT_EN), application address considered as SBL_START_ADDR or 0x0

bitfields.name: BOOT_ATTEST_SKIP
bitfields.description: Boot time attestation record generation

values.name: Enabled
values.description: Not skipped

values.name: Disabled
values.description: Skipped

registers.description: This field is reserved for internal use

registers.name: PG_RECORD_ADDR
registers.description: This is the address where the prove-genuinity record is dumped. If set as zero, then use the 0x28004800 RAM address as the default
